permitted_scopes.description: Scopes the tenant delegates to this skill (least-privilege gate).
permitted_scopes.title: Permitted Scopes

version.description: Version of the skill to install.
version.title: Version

collaboration_id.description: Unique identifier for the shared ledger.
collaboration_id.title: Collaboration Id

manifest.description: Skill manifest fields: skill_id, version, publisher_agent_id, publisher_public_key, required_scopes, declared_tools, description.
manifest.title: Manifest

signature.description: Ed25519 hex signature over canonical manifest bytes.
signature.title: Signature

token.description: The JWT to revoke (by its jti).
token.title: Token

overlap_ttl_seconds.description: Overlap window in seconds. During this window the old key stays verifiable so pre-rotation tokens remain valid. Defaults to key_rotation.overlap_ttl_seconds from config (typically equal to auth.token_ttl_seconds).
overlap_ttl_seconds.title: Overlap Ttl Seconds

tenant_id.description: The tenant for which to issue a JWT.
tenant_id.title: Tenant Id

get.description:
Policy denial statistics for the requesting tenant.

Counts steps with status "denied" across all runs without replaying
the evidence ledger. Response includes total_steps, denied_steps,
deny_rate (0.0–1.0, rounded to 3dp), and a per-run breakdown.

schema.title: Response Admin Deny Rate Admin Policy Deny Rate Get

get.description:
List all governed runs for the requesting tenant with summary fields.

Query params:
  status — optional filter by run status string (e.g. "completed", "halted").

Response shape:
  {"tenant_id": "...", "total": N, "runs": [{summary}, ...]}

Does not call analytics() — projection is O(runs), not O(evidence).

schema.title: Response Admin List Runs Admin Runs Get

get.description:
Combined service-level summary across all tenants (admin overview).

Aggregates total runs, active/completed/halted counts, total cost, and
unique tenant count. Auth protection is inherited from get_tenant, so this
requires a valid token when auth is enabled.

Response shape:
  {"service": "aetheros-control-plane", "total_runs": N, ...}

schema.title: Response Admin Summary Admin Summary Get

get.description:
Budget summary for a specific tenant.

The requesting tenant must match target_tenant_id — cross-tenant access
is forbidden (HTTP 403). Response includes total budget allocated, total
spent, remaining, and run counts by status.

schema.title: Response Admin Budget Admin Tenants  Target Tenant Id  Budget Get

get.description:
Paginated, filterable audit event export from the evidence ledger (Phase 19).

Returns a JSON page of normalised ``AuditEvent`` records, suitable for
SIEM ingestion (Splunk, Datadog, Elastic, Azure Sentinel). Filters:
  ``event_type`` — exact match on event type (e.g. ``tool.invoked``)
  ``since``      — ISO-8601 or Unix epoch lower bound (inclusive)
  ``until``      — ISO-8601 or Unix epoch upper bound (exclusive)
  ``actor``      — exact match on actor id
  ``offset``     — zero-based pagination offset
  ``limit``      — page size (capped at audit.max_page_size in config)

HTTP 403 when audit.enabled = False (default, backward-compatible).
Schema follows OCSF v1.0 (CISA 2022) field conventions.

schema.title: Response Audit Events Audit Events Get

get.description:
Lightweight audit event-count summary across all runs for a tenant (Phase 19).

Returns event type counts, actor counts, and the audit window (earliest/latest
timestamps) without paying the cost of a full event export. Suitable for
SIEM health checks and dashboard widgets.

HTTP 403 when audit.enabled = False (default, backward-compatible).

schema.title: Response Audit Summary Audit Summary Get

get.description:
Publish per-tenant Ed25519 public keys as an RFC 7517 JWK Set.

Verify-only material — never contains private keys. A downstream verifier
selects the right key by matching a token's ``kid`` header to a JWK ``kid``.
Under HS256 (the default) there are no asymmetric keys, so this returns an
empty key set. Always unprotected: public keys are meant to be public.

Phase 18: during a rotation overlap window, both ACTIVE and RETIRING keys
appear in the JWKS so tokens issued before and after the rotation validate
simultaneously.

Return the key registry state for a tenant (EdDSA only, Phase 18).

Lists all key versions (ACTIVE, RETIRING, EXPIRED) with their lifecycle
timestamps. Suitable for ops inspection and rotation audit trails.

HTTP 400 if auth.algorithm is not EdDSA.
HTTP 403 if key_rotation.enabled = False in config.

schema.title: Response Get Key Info Auth Keys  Tenant Id  Get

delete.description:
Emergency: immediately expire all RETIRING keys for a tenant (Phase 18).

This is a break-glass operation that invalidates all currently-retiring keys
without waiting for the overlap window to expire. Tokens signed with the
retired keys will be rejected immediately. The current ACTIVE key is unaffected.

Use this when a key compromise is detected and immediate invalidation of all
pre-rotation tokens is required.

HTTP 400 if auth.algorithm is not EdDSA.
HTTP 403 if key_rotation.enabled = False in config.

schema.title: Response Emergency Retire Keys Auth Keys  Tenant Id  Retire Delete

post.description:
Rotate the Ed25519 signing key for a tenant (EdDSA only, Phase 18).

Atomically generates a new Ed25519 keypair, moves the current ACTIVE key to
RETIRING (verifiable but no longer signing), and stamps the new versioned kid
on all subsequent tokens for this tenant.

Returns the new ``kid``, the old (retiring) ``kid``, and the overlap window
duration so the caller can audit the rotation event.

HTTP 400 if auth.algorithm is not EdDSA (key rotation is meaningless for HS256).
HTTP 403 if key_rotation.enabled = False in config.

schema.title: Response Rotate Key Auth Keys  Tenant Id  Rotate Post

post.description: Revoke a JWT by its jti. The token will be rejected on all future requests.

schema.title: Response Revoke Token Auth Revoke Post

post.description:
Issue a signed JWT for a tenant.

The caller must supply the correct ``admin_secret`` (from config). On success,
returns ``{"token": "<jwt>", "token_type": "bearer", "expires_in": <seconds>}``.
When auth is disabled this endpoint still works — useful for pre-provisioning
tokens before flipping auth on.

schema.title: Response Issue Token Auth Token Post

get.description: List all collaborations visible to the requesting tenant.

post.description: Open (or retrieve) a tenant-scoped shared ledger for multi-agent collaboration.

get.description: Return a collaboration's full state and tamper-evident shared ledger.

schema.title: Response Get Collaboration Collaborations  Collaboration Id  Get

post.description: Admit an agent to a collaboration, verifying its capability lease signature.

schema.title: Response Admit Agent Collaborations  Collaboration Id  Admit Post

post.description: Append an attributed, tamper-evident entry to the collaboration's shared ledger.

schema.title: Response Contribute Collaborations  Collaboration Id  Contribute Post

get.description:
Return the Prometheus alerting and recording rules for AetherOS.

Returns the raw YAML text of config/alerting/rules.yml so operators
can pipe it directly into their Prometheus configuration:
    curl http://localhost:8765/config/alerting > rules.yml

The YAML follows Prometheus rule group format (prometheus.io/docs 2023).
Recording rules pre-compute the four golden signal rates (deny rate,
completion rate, duration p95/p99, budget throughput). Alerting rules
fire on thresholds derived from the OTEL instruments defined in Phase 20.

schema.title: Response Alerting Rules Config Alerting Get

get.description:
Legacy liveness probe — backward-compatible alias for /health/live.

Returns status="ok" to maintain backward compatibility with Phase 5-20
tests that call GET /health and expect {"status": "ok", ...}.

Legacy liveness probe — backward-compatible alias for /health/live.

Returns status="ok" to maintain backward compatibility with Phase 5-20
tests that call GET /health and expect {"status": "ok", ...}.
Deep probe — full dependency + data-integrity check.
Liveness probe — always returns 200 if the process is alive.
Readiness probe — checks critical dependencies.
List all governed skills available in the marketplace catalog.

schema.title: Response Marketplace Catalog Marketplace Catalog Get

get.description: List all skills installed for the requesting tenant.

schema.title: Response Marketplace Installed Marketplace Installed Get

post.description:
Publish a signed skill to the marketplace catalog.

The Ed25519 signature must verify over the manifest's canonical bytes.
Raises 400 if the signature is invalid.

schema.title: Response Publish Skill Marketplace Skills Post

post.description:
Install a marketplace skill under the full governance gate for a tenant.

Verifies Ed25519 origin, enforces least-privilege scope delegation, and
evaluates constitutional supremacy. Returns 404 if skill not in catalog,
400 if any governance check fails.

schema.title: Response Install Skill Marketplace Skills  Skill Id  Install Post

get.description:
Prometheus text-format scrape endpoint (OpenMetrics v1.0.0).

Returns all aetheros.* OTEL instruments plus standard Python process
and GC metrics. Responds with Content-Type text/plain; version=0.0.4.

HTTP 404 when prometheus.enabled = False (default).
HTTP 503 when prometheus_client is not installed.

delete.description:
Remove a terminal (completed/halted) run from the service registry.

Returns 204 No Content on success. Active runs must be cancelled first.

schema.title: Response Get Run Runs  Run Id  Get
Response Advance Runs  Run Id  Advance Post

post.description: Cancel a non-terminal run. The cancellation is recorded in the evidence ledger.

schema.title: Response Cancel Run Runs  Run Id  Cancel Post
Response Evidence Runs  Run Id  Evidence Get
Response Resume Runs  Run Id  Resume Post

get.description: Signed Tree Head over a run's evidence ledger; optional ?leaf=N inclusion proof.

schema.title: Response Transparency Runs  Run Id  Transparency Get

get.description: Append-only consistency proof from ?first_size=M to the current ledger size.

schema.title: Response Transparency Consistency Runs  Run Id  Transparency Consistency Get

get.description:
Signed tree head plus independent witness cosignatures (split-view defense).

Returns the STH, the gathered witness cosignatures, the panel size/threshold,
and a `trustworthy` flag that is true once `threshold` distinct witnesses have
cosigned the head along a consistent, append-only history.

schema.title: Response Transparency Cosigned Runs  Run Id  Transparency Cosigned Get
Response Get Tenant By Id Tenants  Tenant Id  Get
